title: Probabilistic Text Generation via Softmax
explanation: Adjusting the temperature of the softmax function changes how deterministic or creative the model's next-token predictions are.

title: Self-Attention Mechanism
explanation: Visualize how query and key vectors interact via dot products to determine attention weights between tokens.

title: Character Prediction Softmax
explanation: The model outputs logits for each possible next character, which are converted into probabilities using the softmax function to predict the next character in the sequence.

title: Loss Curve Decay Model
explanation: Visualize how the loss curve decays over training iterations based on the starting loss, decay rate, and final convergence limit.

title: Logits to Probabilities via Softmax
explanation: In a bigram language model, the lookup table outputs raw logits for each token in the vocabulary, which are then converted into a probability distribution using the softmax function.

title: Embedding Lookup as Matrix Multiplication
explanation: An embedding lookup of a token index is mathematically equivalent to multiplying a one-hot encoded vector by the embedding weight matrix.

title: Logits to Probabilities via Softmax
explanation: In a bigram language model, the model outputs raw scores (logits) for each possible next token, which are then converted into probabilities using the softmax function.

title: Softmax and Cross-Entropy Loss
explanation: Visualize how raw model outputs (logits) are converted into probabilities using the softmax function, which is the core component of Cross-Entropy Loss.

title: Softmax and Cross-Entropy Loss
explanation: The softmax function converts raw model outputs (logits) into probabilities. When logits are uniform, the probability distribution is flat, resulting in an expected negative log likelihood of -ln(1/N).

title: Softmax Probability Distribution
explanation: The model outputs raw scores (logits) for each token, which are converted into a probability distribution using the softmax function.

title: Softmax Probability Distribution
explanation: Convert raw model outputs (logits) into a probability distribution for sampling the next token.

title: Softmax and Temperature in Token Generation
explanation: Softmax converts raw model outputs (logits) into a probability distribution, where temperature controls the randomness of the generated text.

title: Learning Rate and Loss Decay
explanation: Visualize how the learning rate controls the speed of convergence and loss reduction over training steps.

title: Logits to Probabilities via Softmax
explanation: The model outputs raw scores (logits) for each possible next character, which are converted into a probability distribution using the softmax function.

title: Softmax Probability Distribution
explanation: The softmax function converts raw model outputs (logits) into a probability distribution over vocabulary tokens, which can be scaled by temperature to control generation randomness.

title: Softmax and Temperature in Text Generation
explanation: Adjusting the temperature scales the logits before softmax, controlling the randomness of the next-token sampling during generation.

title: Causal Aggregation via Matrix Multiplication
explanation: Multiplying by a lower triangular matrix allows us to efficiently compute a running average of past tokens in a sequence.

title: Historical Averaging via Matrix Multiplication
explanation: Multiplying by a normalized lower-triangular matrix computes a running average of token features up to the current time step, representing a simple form of causal attention.

title: Causal Mean as Matrix Multiplication
explanation: Multiplying by a lower-triangular averaging matrix computes a running average of token features up to the current time step.

title: Matrix Multiplication with Uniform Weights
explanation: Multiplying a matrix of ones by another matrix computes the sum of each column for every row.

title: Matrix Multiplication for Cumulative Sums
explanation: Multiplying by a lower-triangular matrix of ones performs a cumulative sum over the rows of the target matrix, which is a foundational step towards causal attention masking.

title: Vectorized Cumulative Average via Matrix Multiplication
explanation: Multiplying by a lower-triangular, row-normalized matrix computes a running average of the rows of the input matrix.

title: Causal Aggregation via Matrix Multiplication
explanation: Multiplying by a normalized lower-triangular matrix computes a running average of past tokens, preventing future information leakage in autoregressive models.

title: Softmax with Attention Masking
explanation: Applying softmax to logits where future tokens are masked with negative values (representing -inf) ensures they receive zero attention probability.

title: Causal Attention Masking and Aggregation
explanation: Multiplying a normalized lower-triangular attention weight matrix with token features aggregates information only from past and current tokens.

title: The tril trick — live numpy from the video
explanation: Karpathy's masked-averaging cell, running as real numpy in your browser. Drag T, edit the code.

cells:
import numpy as np
T = int(T)
tril = np.tril(np.ones((T, T)))
wei = tril / tril.sum(1, keepdims=True)
print(np.round(wei, 3))

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.figure(figsize=(4,3))
plt.imshow(wei, cmap='Greens')
plt.title('attention weights (uniform over past)')
plt.colorbar()
plt.show()

title: Language Model Head Projection
explanation: The language model head projects token embeddings of size n_embd back to the vocabulary size to produce logits for each token.

title: Logits to Probabilities via Softmax
explanation: The language model outputs raw scores (logits) for each token in the vocabulary, which are converted into a probability distribution using the softmax function.

title: Causal Masking via Matrix Multiplication
explanation: Multiplying the lower-triangular attention weight matrix with the input features computes a running average of past tokens, preventing future information leakage.

title: Self-Attention Query-Key Affinity
explanation: Self-attention calculates affinities between tokens by computing the dot product of queries (what a token is looking for) and keys (what a token contains).

title: Query-Key Dot Product Attention
explanation: Affinities are computed by taking the dot product of query vectors with key vectors, determining how much attention each token pays to others.

title: Self-Attention: Query-Key Interaction
explanation: See how query vectors dot-product with key vectors to produce attention weights, which are then masked and normalized via softmax.

title: Self-Attention: Query-Key Affinity
explanation: Compute the raw attention weights (affinities) by taking the dot product of query vectors with key vectors, followed by masking and softmax normalization.

title: Self-Attention Query-Key Interaction
explanation: Compute attention weights by taking the dot product of queries (Q) and keys (K), showing how nodes in a sequence determine how much information to aggregate from each other.

title: Self-Attention Query-Key Interaction
explanation: Compute attention weights by taking the dot product of query vectors (Q) and key vectors (K), showing how tokens focus on each other.

title: Self-Attention with Causal Masking
explanation: Self-attention computes attention weights by taking the dot product of queries and keys, which are then masked to prevent looking at future tokens.

title: Effect of Scaling on Softmax (Scaled Dot-Product Attention)
explanation: Scaling logits by dividing by sqrt(d_k) (modeled here as temperature) reduces their variance, preventing the softmax from saturating into a one-hot distribution at initialization.

title: Self-Attention Score Computation
explanation: Compute attention weights by taking the dot product of queries (Q) and keys (K), scaling by the square root of the head dimension, and applying softmax.

title: Self-Attention Query-Key Interaction
explanation: Self-attention computes similarity scores between queries (Q) and keys (K) to determine how much focus each token should place on other tokens in the sequence.

title: Multi-Head Self-Attention: Single Head Query-Key Matching
explanation: In multi-head attention, each head operates on a lower-dimensional subspace to compute attention weights by taking the dot product of queries (Q) and keys (K).

title: Linear Layer with ReLU Activation
explanation: Visualize how the weight (w) and bias (b) affect the output of a single neuron with a ReLU activation function, which introduces non-linearity to the network.

title: Self-Attention Mechanism
explanation: Self-attention allows tokens to communicate by computing attention weights from the dot product of queries (Q) and keys (K).

title: Residual Connections and Identity Mapping
explanation: Visualize how adding a residual connection (F(x) + residual * x) preserves the identity mapping, ensuring stable gradient flow even when the learned transformation F(x) is highly non-linear or saturated.

title: Residual Connection Gradient Highway
explanation: Visualize how residual connections preserve the identity path (y = x) at initialization when block contribution (a) is near zero, ensuring unimpeded gradient flow.

title: Linear Layer with ReLU Activation
explanation: Visualize how a single neuron with a weight, bias, and ReLU non-linearity transforms its input.

title: Multi-Head Attention: Query-Key Matching
explanation: In the Transformer architecture, attention weights are computed by taking the dot product of Query (Q) and Key (K) vectors to determine how much focus to place on other parts of the input sequence.

title: Layer Normalization Transform
explanation: LayerNorm normalizes features to have a zero mean and unit variance, then scales and shifts them using learnable parameters gamma and beta.

title: Softmax and Temperature in Text Generation
explanation: Adjust the temperature to see how it scales the logits before softmax, controlling the randomness of the model's next-character predictions.

title: Visualizing Attention Weights
explanation: Interactive calculation of attention weights by computing the dot product of query and key vectors.

title: Bidirectional Self-Attention in the Encoder
explanation: In the Transformer encoder, all tokens can freely attend to each other without a mask, computing attention weights via query-key dot products.

title: GELU Activation Function Approximation
explanation: The Gaussian Error Linear Unit (GELU) scales inputs by their cumulative distribution function, providing a smooth, non-monotonic alternative to ReLU.

title: Multi-Head Attention: Query-Key Dot Product
explanation: Visualize how query and key vectors interact via dot products to produce attention weights before softmax is applied.

title: GELU Activation Function
explanation: GELU weights inputs by their value under a cumulative Gaussian distribution, smoothing the hard threshold of ReLU.

title: Softmax Probability Distribution
explanation: Converting raw model outputs (logits) into a probability distribution allows us to sample the next token.

title: Transformer Parameter Scaling
explanation: Visualize how the total parameter count (in millions) scales quadratically with the model dimension (d_model, on the x-axis) and linearly with the number of layers (L).
